what_it_does: Defines an HTML page that loads shared styles and runtime scripts, fetches layout and decision data, selects the substrate-home decision record, renders a composed decision-card view, and appends it to the mount element.
observations: The page includes Plus Jakarta Sans and IBM Plex Mono from Google Fonts.
It loads /surface/styles/piece.css and /surface/styles/phone.css before a small inline style block.
The inline CSS sets the body background to var(--warm) and makes #mount a 100vh inline-size container.
The script hard-codes ID as substrate-home and address as decision://global/substrate-home.
It selects layouts.archetypes["decision-card"] and the decision record matching the fixed ID.
The rendered archetype receives identity data merged with address and a pending state.
The resolve function supplies explanation_source from rec.legibility.why, supplies state as pending, and otherwise returns an empty string.
The output is wrapped with DNA.renderExplained and given the dv-c class before being appended to #mount.
standouts: The page is a file-local rendered instance driven by one fixed decision ID.
The interface render and slide explanation render are composed together in the same script.
The code repeats the pending state in both identity data and the resolve function.
conventions: Uses a minimal static HTML shell with runtime-driven rendering.
Keeps page-specific configuration inline while relying on shared CSS and runtime scripts.
Uses an async immediately invoked function for setup.
Uses a fixed content ID to generate one page instance.
concerns: There is no local error handling for failed fetches, missing layout data, or a missing decision record.
The hard-coded pending state appears in two places within the render configuration.
notes: This file is a generated-style page shell for rendering the substrate-home decision card as a composed explained view.
questions: What should the page display if the substrate-home record is missing?
Is pending always the intended state for this rendered page?
What values besides explanation_source and state can the resolve function be asked to supply?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose in the comment matches the actual structure: the file builds a composed decision view containing the interface and explanatory slide layer.
intention_signals: It seems intended to turn a decision record into a self-contained composed view where the decision interface and its explanation are visible together.
novelty: The page treats a decision card as both an interface and an explained slide, rendered from archetype and content data rather than static markup.
contribution: This file contributes a concrete composed-page wrapper that binds a decision substrate ID, shared archetype layout, decision content, visual device configuration, and explanation source into one mountable rendered view.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML page that renders the substrate-home decision record as a composed decision-card view using shared DNA layout, content, runtime scripts, and a fixed pending state.
intention_for_embedding: This was for presenting one decision substrate as a contained interface with an interpretive explanation layer in a generated page shell.
